steps.title: Recognize the exponential pattern
steps.content:
The phrase "increases by a factor of 4" for each unit increase in $x$ means $y$ is multiplied by $4$ each time $x$ increases by $1$. This is exponential growth of the form:
$$y = a \cdot b^x$$
where $b = 4$ is the growth factor and $a$ is the initial value.

steps.title: Use the initial condition
steps.content:
When $x = 0$, $y = 200$. Substituting:
$$200 = a \cdot 4^0 = a \cdot 1 = a$$
So $a = 200$, giving us:
$$y = 200(4)^x$$

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A: $y=4(x)^{200}$ — this is a power function with $x$ as the base, not exponential growth; at $x=0$ this gives $y=0$, not $200$
  • B: $y=4(200)^x$ — at $x=0$ this gives $y=4$, not $200$; also has growth factor $200$, not $4$
  • C: $y=200(x)^4$ — power function; at $x=0$ this gives $y=0$, not $200$
  • D: $y=200(4)^x$ — at $x=0$: $y=200(1)=200$ ✓; at $x=1$: $y=200(4)=800$, which is $200 \times 4$ ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
